summary: Joel provides a brief status update on the community-driven effort to translate Joel on Software articles into multiple languages. He notes that new translations are available in over ten languages and acknowledges that he sometimes delays responding to volunteer translators for up to a week. He puts out a call for copy editors in several languages where translations are stuck in the queue.
key_points: Joel on Software articles are being translated into numerous languages by volunteer contributors
Joel batches translation-related emails and processes them weekly rather than responding immediately
Several translations are blocked in the queue due to a lack of copy editors for specific languages
money_quotes: As my faithful volunteers have learned it sometimes takes me a week to respond to their email; I usually let all the translation-related email pile up and then work through it once a week.
key_insight: A brief housekeeping update on the volunteer-driven internationalization of Joel on Software, notable mainly as an example of community-powered content translation in the early blogging era.
